fields.name: Document Type
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the type of document that you are about to create.
The purchase header forms the basis for all the document types related to the Purchase & Payables application area that have not yet been posted, so the program must label what it is you are setting up, for example, an invoice or credit memo.
The program automatically fills in this field based on what you have chosen on the Purchases & Payables menu.

fields.name: Buy-from Vendor No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the vendor who delivers the products.

fields.name: No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the involved entry or record, according to the specified number series.

fields.name: Pay-to Vendor No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the vendor that you received the invoice from.

fields.name: Pay-to Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the vendor sending the invoice.

fields.name: Pay-to Name 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional part of the name of the vendor who you receive the invoice or credit memo from.

fields.name: Pay-to Address
fields.tooltip: Specifies the address of the vendor that you received the invoice from.

fields.name: Pay-to Address 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional part of the address of the vendor that the invoice was received from.

fields.name: Pay-to City
fields.tooltip: Specifies the city of the vendor sending the invoice.

fields.name: Pay-to Contact
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the person to contact about an order from this vendor.

fields.name: Your Reference
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's reference.

fields.name: Ship-to Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for another delivery address than the vendor's own address, which is entered by default.

fields.name: Ship-to Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the company at the address to which you want the items in the purchase order to be shipped.

fields.name: Ship-to Name 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional part of the name of the company at the address to which you want the items in the purchase order to be shipped.

fields.name: Ship-to Address
fields.tooltip: Specifies the address that you want the items on the purchase document to be shipped to.

fields.name: Ship-to Address 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional part of the address that items on the purchase order were shipped to, as a drop shipment.

fields.name: Ship-to City
fields.tooltip: Specifies the city the items in the purchase order will be shipped to.

fields.name: Ship-to Contact
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of a contact person for the address where the items in the purchase order should be shipped.

fields.name: Order Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date the order was created. The order date is also used to determine the prices and discounts on the document.

fields.name: Posting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the posting date of the record.

fields.name: Expected Receipt Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date you expect to receive the items on the purchase document.

fields.name: Posting Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies additional posting information for the document. After you post the document, the description can add detail to vendor and customer ledger entries.

fields.name: Payment Terms Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a formula that calculates the payment due date, payment discount date, and payment discount amount.

fields.name: Due Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies when the related sales invoice must be paid.

fields.name: Payment Discount %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the payment discount percentage that is granted if you pay on or before the date entered in the Pmt. Discount Date field. The discount percentage is specified in the Payment Terms Code field.

fields.name: Pmt. Discount Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date on which the amount in the entry must be paid for a payment discount to be granted.

fields.name: Shipment Method Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the delivery conditions of the related shipment, such as free on board (FOB).

fields.name: Location Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the location from where the items are shipped. When you select the vendor and the vendor has a location assigned, the value is taken from the Vendor card. If the vendor has no location, but a Responsibility Center is populated, the location code is taken from the Responsibility Center. If neither is specified, the value is taken from Company Information. This field acts as the default location for new lines. You can update the location code for individual lines as needed.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 1 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 1, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Shortcut Dimension 2 Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for Shortcut Dimension 2, which is one of two global dimension codes that you set up in the General Ledger Setup window.

fields.name: Vendor Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's market type to link business transactions to.

fields.name: Currency Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the currency that is used on the entry.

fields.name: Currency Factor
fields.tooltip: This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: Prices Including VAT
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the Unit Price and Line Amount fields on document lines should be shown with or without VAT.

fields.name: Invoice Disc. Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code that will be used to find the invoice discount you receive from the vendor.
The Invoice Disc. Code is retrieved from the Vendor table when you fill in the Pay-to Vendor No. field.
The invoice discount will be based on the total invoice amount, but it will be allocated to the lines relative to the amount on each. The discountamount is calculated when you choose the Calculate Invoice Discount. For more information, see Calculating Invoice Discounts on Purchases.
If there is a check mark in the Calc. Inv. Discount field in the Purchases & Payables Setup window, the amount will be calculated automatically. For more information about when automatic calculation occurs, see Calc. Inv. Discount.

fields.name: Language Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the language to be used on printouts for this document.

fields.name: Format Region
fields.tooltip: Specifies the format to be used on printouts for this document.

fields.name: Purchaser Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies which purchaser is assigned to the vendor.

fields.name: Order Class
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a number which can later be used for statistical purposes.
This field is not used elsewhere in the program.

fields.name: Comment
fields.tooltip:
Specifies whether a comment has been entered for this purchase header.
To view a comment or create a new one, click Related Information, point to Order, and then click Comments. The Purch. Comment Sheet window will appear. You can see the existing comments and create new ones.
If you are in a quote, invoice or credit memo, the name of the menu option will be Quote, Invoice or Credit Memo instead of Order.
You can also enter a comment in the Notes FactBox.

fields.name: No. Printed
fields.tooltip:
Specifies how many times a quote or order has been printed.
When a quote is changed to an order, this field is set to 0.

fields.name: On Hold
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the related entry represents an unpaid invoice for which either a payment suggestion, a reminder, or a finance charge memo exists.

fields.name: Applies-to Doc. Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of the posted document that this document or journal line will be applied to when you post, for example to register payment.

fields.name: Applies-to Doc. No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the posted document that this document or journal line will be applied to when you post, for example to register payment.

fields.name: Bal. Account No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number of a general ledger account or the bank account that the invoice should be offset against (for cash purchases, for example). Which account type you can choose, is decided in the field Bal. Account Type.
You can also use a search name to enter an account. If you want to enter the cash account, for example, but cannot remember the account number, you can enter the word "cash" and press ENTER. The program will enter the correct account number. This assumes that you have set up a search name for the account.

fields.name: Receive
fields.tooltip:
Specifies if quantities on the purchase document have been posted as received.
The field contains Yes if the value in the Quantity Received field is higher than zero.
The field is used internally to signal that warehouse activities, such as a put-away, can start.

fields.name: Invoice
fields.tooltip: Uses this field when posting.

fields.name: Print Posted Documents
fields.tooltip: This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sum of amounts on all the lines in the document. This will include invoice discounts.

fields.name: Amount Including VAT
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sum of amounts, including VAT, on all the lines in the document. This will include invoice discounts.

fields.name: Receiving No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number that will be assigned to the receipt when the purchase header is posted.
When you post the purchase header, the program will automatically use the next number from the number series in the Receiving No. Series field unless you manually enter a number.
If you manually enter a number, it will not cause a gap in the number series.

fields.name: Posting No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number of the posted invoice or credit memo that will be created if you post the purchase header.
When you post the purchase header, the program will automatically enter the next number from the number series in the Posting No. Series field unless you manually enter a number.
If you manually enter a number, it will not cause a gap in the number series.

fields.name: Last Receiving No.
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally. Tracks the last posted receipt number that was used.

fields.name: Last Posting No.
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally. Tracks the last posted invoice number that was used.

fields.name: Vendor Order No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's order number.

fields.name: Vendor Shipment No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's shipment number. It is inserted in the corresponding field on the source document during posting.

fields.name: Vendor Invoice No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the document number of the original document you received from the vendor. You can require the document number for posting, or let it be optional. By default, it's required, so that this document references the original. Making document numbers optional removes a step from the posting process. For example, if you attach the original invoice as a PDF, you might not need to enter the document number. To specify whether document numbers are required, in the Purchases & Payables Setup window, select or clear the Ext. Doc. No. Mandatory field.

fields.name: Vendor Cr. Memo No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number that the vendor uses for the purchase order.

fields.name: VAT Registration No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the vendor's VAT registration number.
The program automatically retrieves the number from the Vendor table when you fill in the Buy-from Vendor No. field. The field is blank if no VAT registration number has been entered on the vendor's card.
This number is required if you must report EU VAT.
Normally, you should not change the contents of this field.

fields.name: Sell-to Customer No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the customer that the items are shipped to directly from your vendor, as a drop shipment.

fields.name: Reason Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the reason code, a supplementary source code that enables you to trace the document.

fields.name: Gen. Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for the general business posting group of the vendor.
The program automatically retrieves the code from the Vendor table when you fill in the Buy-from Vendor No. field.
The code specifies to which general business posting group this particular vendor belongs. The general business posting groups can be set up to group vendors by geographical area (Domestic, EU countries/regions, Overseas, and so on), type of business, or to distinguish between private entities and governmental agencies.
The program uses the business code, together with the Gen. Product Posting Group and Gen. Posting Type fields, to determine certain trade conditions. When you post transactions that involve this vendor, the program looks for the business code, in combination with the general product posting group code, in the General Posting Setup, where the G/L accounts to which the program posts purchases, discount amounts, COGS and inventory adjustments are specified.
To see the general business posting groups in the Gen. Business Posting Group table, click the field.
Normally, you should not change the contents of this field but it may be necessary in special cases where the default code is not appropriate.

fields.name: Transaction Type
fields.tooltip: Specifies the type of transaction that the document represents, for the purpose of reporting to INTRASTAT.

fields.name: Transport Method
fields.tooltip: Specifies the transport method, for the purpose of reporting to INTRASTAT.

fields.name: VAT Country/Region Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the vendor's VAT country/region code.
The program automatically retrieves the country/region code from the Vendor table when you fill in the Buy-from Vendor No. field.
Information about the country/region code will be used for INTRASTAT and EU-VAT reporting.
The country/region codes are set up in the Country/Region table.

fields.name: Buy-from Vendor Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the vendor who delivers the products.

fields.name: Buy-from Vendor Name 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional part of the name of the vendor that you’re buying from.

fields.name: Buy-from Address
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor's buy-from address.

fields.name: Buy-from Address 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies an additional part of the vendor's buy-from address.

fields.name: Buy-from City
fields.tooltip: Specifies the city of the vendor who ships the items.

fields.name: Buy-from Contact
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the person to contact about shipment of the item from this vendor.

fields.name: Pay-to Post Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the postal code.

fields.name: Pay-to County
fields.tooltip: Specifies the state, province or county as a part of the address.

fields.name: Pay-to Country/Region Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the country/region code of the vendor on the purchase document.

fields.name: Buy-from Post Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the postal code.

fields.name: Buy-from County
fields.tooltip: Specifies the state, province or county as a part of the address.

fields.name: Buy-from Country/Region Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the country or region of the address.

fields.name: Ship-to Post Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the postal code.

fields.name: Ship-to County
fields.tooltip: Specifies the state, province or county as a part of the address.

fields.name: Ship-to Country/Region Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the country/region code of the address that you want the items on the purchase document to be shipped to.

fields.name: Bal. Account Type
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the type of balancing account used in this document.
To see the two possible account types, click the field. You can choose G/L Account or Bank Account. The balancing account type determines which accounts can be entered in the Bal. Account No. field.

fields.name: Order Address Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the order address code linked to the relevant vendor's order address.

fields.name: Entry Point
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the port of entry where the items pass into your country/region, for reporting to Intrastat.

fields.name: Correction
fields.tooltip: Specifies the entry as a corrective entry. You can use the field if you need to post a corrective entry to a vendor account. If you place a check mark in this field when posting a corrective entry, the system will post a negative debit instead of a credit or a negative credit instead of a debit. Correction flag does not affect how inventory reconciled with general ledger.

fields.name: Document Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date when the related document was created.

fields.name: Area
fields.tooltip: Specifies the destination country or region for the purpose of Intrastat reporting.

fields.name: Transaction Specification
fields.tooltip: Specifies a specification of the document's transaction, for the purpose of reporting to INTRASTAT.

fields.name: Payment Method Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies how to make payment, such as with bank transfer, cash, or check.

fields.name: No. Series
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the number series that was used to assign the purchase document's number.

fields.name: Posting No. Series
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for the number series that will be used to assign a number to the purchase document when it is posted.
The program automatically copied the number series code from the relevant field in the Purchases & Payables Setup table:

If the document is an invoice, order or quote, the program copied the number series code from the Posted Invoice Nos. field.


If the document is a credit memo, the program copied the number series code from the Posted Credit Memo Nos. field.

fields.name: Receiving No. Series
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for the number series that will be used to assign a number to the receipt that will be created from the purchase document.
The program automatically copied the number series code from the Posted Receipt Nos. field in the Purchases & Payables Setup table. You can change the code.

fields.name: Tax Area Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the tax area that is used to calculate and post sales tax.

fields.name: Tax Liable
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the customer or vendor is liable for sales tax.

fields.name: VAT Bus. Posting Group
fields.tooltip: Specifies the VAT specification of the involved customer or vendor to link transactions made for this record with the appropriate general ledger account according to the VAT posting setup.

fields.name: Applies-to ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ID of entries that will be applied to when you choose the Apply Entries action.

fields.name: VAT Base Discount %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the percentage to use to discount the VAT element if a payment discount is to be posted. The value is validated against the maximum possible VAT Tolerance % field value in the General Ledger Setup. The maximum value cannot be overridden with the exception of a credit note where you can manually enter the value used for the payment discount.

fields.name: Status
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the record is open, is waiting to be approved, has been invoiced for prepayment, or has been released to the next stage of processing.

fields.name: Invoice Discount Calculation
fields.tooltip:
Specifies how to calculate the discount for an invoice. You can select from the following options by clicking the field:

Option

Meaning


None

The program will not calculate an invoice discount.


%

The program will calculate the invoice discount as a percentage of the invoice amount.


Amount

The program will calculate the invoice discount by subtracting a specified amount from the invoice amount.

fields.name: Invoice Discount Value
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the value by the invoice discount calculation method you chose in the Invoice Discount Calculation field.
If you chose the option None, the field will be empty.
If you chose %, this field will display the percentage that the program will use to calculate the invoice discount.
If you chose Amount, this field will display the amount that the program will use to calculate the invoice discount.

fields.name: Send IC Document
fields.tooltip: Specifies that an intercompany transaction is created in the intercompany outbox when you post the document or use the Send IC Document function.

fields.name: IC Status
fields.tooltip:
Specifies whether a copy of the document has been created in the intercompany outbox.
The field can have the following values.


Status


Description


New

There is no copy of the document in the outbox.


qu


There is a copy of the document in the outbox. It has not been sent yet.


Sent

A copy of the document has been sent from the outbox.

fields.name: Buy-from IC Partner Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the buy-from vendor's intercompany partner code if the vendor on the document is an intercompany partner.
If the field contains an intercompany partner code, then the document is an intercompany document. A copy of the document will be sent to the intercompany outbox if the Send IC Document field contains a check mark. Furthermore, when you post the document, the resulting entries will be marked as intercompany entries.

fields.name: Pay-to IC Partner Code
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the pay-to vendor's intercompany partner code if the pay-to vendor on the document is an intercompany partner.
If the field contains an intercompany partner code, then the document is an intercompany document. A copy of the document will be sent to the intercompany outbox if the Send IC Document field contains a check mark. Furthermore, when you post the document, the resulting entries will be marked as intercompany entries.

fields.name: IC Direction
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether you are sending the document to an intercompany partner or whether you received the document from an intercompany partner if the document is an intercompany document. Sending a document is outgoing and receiving a document is incoming.

fields.name: Prepayment No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number that will be assigned to the posted prepayment invoice for this purchase order.
When you post the purchase prepayment invoice, the program will automatically use the next number from the number series in the Posted Prepmt. Invoice Nos. Series field unless you manually enter a number.
If you manually enter a number, it will not cause a gap in the number series.

fields.name: Last Prepayment No.
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally. Tracks the last posted prepayment invoice number that was used for this purchase order.

fields.name: Prepmt. Cr. Memo No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number that will be assigned to the posted prepayment credit memo for this purchase order.
When you post the purchase prepayment credit memo, the program will automatically use the next number from the number series in the Posted Prepmt. Cr. Memo Nos. field unless you manually enter a number.
If you manually enter a number, it will not cause a gap in the number series.

fields.name: Last Prepmt. Cr. Memo No.
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally. Tracks the last posted prepayment credit memo number that was used for this purchase order.

fields.name: Prepayment %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the prepayment percentage to use to calculate the prepayment for purchase.

fields.name: Prepayment No. Series
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for the number series that will be used to assign a number to prepayment invoices posted from this purchase order
The program automatically copies the number series code from the Posted Prepmt. Invoice Nos. field in the Purchases & Payables Setup table.

fields.name: Compress Prepayment
fields.tooltip: Specifies that prepayments on the purchase order are combined if they have the same general ledger account for prepayments or the same dimensions.

fields.name: Prepayment Due Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies when the prepayment invoice for this purchase order is due.

fields.name: Prepmt. Cr. Memo No. Series
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for the number series that will be used to assign a number to prepayment credit memos posted from this purchase order.
The program automatically copies the number series code from the Posted Prepmt. Cr. Memo Nos. field in the Purchases & Payables Setup table.

fields.name: Prepmt. Posting Description
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the posting description that you want to use on the entries that will be created when you post a prepayment invoice for this purchase order.
If you leave the field blank, the description on the posted entries will be "Prepayment Invoice, Order" and the order number that the prepayment invoice is valid for.
The posting description appears on vendor and G/L entries.

fields.name: Prepmt. Pmt. Discount Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the last date the vendor can pay the prepayment invoice and still receive a payment discount on the prepayment amount.

fields.name: Prepmt. Payment Terms Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code that represents the payment terms for prepayment invoices related to the purchase document.

fields.name: Prepmt. Payment Discount %
fields.tooltip: Specifies the payment discount percent granted on the prepayment if the vendor pays on or before the date entered in the Prepmt. Pmt. Discount Date field.

fields.name: Quote No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the quote number for the purchase order.

fields.name: Job Queue Status
fields.tooltip: Specifies the status of a job queue entry that handles the posting of purchase credit memos.

fields.name: Job Queue Entry ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ID of the job queue entry.

fields.name: Incoming Document Entry No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the incoming document that this purchase document is created for.

fields.name: Creditor No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the vendor who sent the purchase invoice.

fields.name: Payment Reference
fields.tooltip: Specifies the payment of the purchase invoice.

fields.name: Invoice Received Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date when the related document was received.

fields.name: Journal Templ. Name
fields.tooltip: Specifies the name of the journal template in which the purchase header is to be posted.

fields.name: VAT Reporting Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date used to include entries on VAT reports in a VAT period. This is either the date that the document was created or posted, depending on your setting on the General Ledger Setup page.

fields.name: Ship-to Phone No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the telephone number of the company's shipping address.

fields.name: A. Rcd. Not Inv. Ex. VAT (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the amount excluding VAT for the items on the order that have been received but are not yet invoiced.

fields.name: Amt. Rcd. Not Invoiced (LCY)
fields.tooltip: Specifies the sum, in LCY, for items that have been received but have not yet been invoiced. The value in the Amt. Rcd. Not Invoiced (LCY) field is used for entries in the Purchase Line table of document type Order to calculate and update the contents of this field.

fields.name: Dimension Set ID
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a reference to a combination of dimension values. The actual values are stored in the Dimension Set Entry table.


Additional Information


The dimension set ID is not edited directly by users. The value of the field is calculated with the dimension values that users select in the Edit Dimension Set Entries window.


The value 0 represents an empty dimension set.

fields.name: Remit-to Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the vendor's remit address for this order.

fields.name: Invoice Discount Amount
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the amount that is calculated and shown in the Invoice Discount Amount field at the bottom of purchase invoices and posted purchase invoices.
Specifies the amount that is deducted from the value shown in the Total Amount Incl. VAT field.
If you have set up invoice discounts for the vendor, then the specified percentage value is automatically inserted in the Vendor Invoice Discount % field if the criteria are met. The calculated discount amount is inserted in the Invoice Discount Amount field, but you can change it manually. For more information, see How to: Set Up Invoice Discount Terms.

fields.name: No. of Archived Versions
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of archived versions for this document.

fields.name: Doc. No. Occurrence
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number of times the "No." value has been used in the number series.
This number, in combination with the document number, ensures that you always maintain a unique number for the archived document. This is convenient if, for example, you have changed the number series and, as a result, a conflict of numbers arises.

fields.name: Campaign No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of the campaign that the document is linked to.

fields.name: Buy-from Contact No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of contact person of the vendor's buy-from.

fields.name: Pay-to Contact No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the number of contact person of the vendor's buy-from.

fields.name: Buy-from Vendor Templ. Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code for the template to create a new vendors

fields.name: Responsibility Center
fields.tooltip: Specifies the code of the responsibility center, such as a distribution hub, that is associated with the involved user, company, customer, or vendor.

fields.name: Completely Received
fields.tooltip: Specifies if all the items on the order have been shipped or, in the case of inbound items, completely received.

fields.name: Posting from Whse. Ref.
fields.tooltip: This object supports the Microsoft Dynamics NAV infrastructure and is intended only for internal use.

fields.name: Location Filter
fields.tooltip: Specifies the location filter.

fields.name: Requested Receipt Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date that you want the vendor to deliver your order. The field is used to calculate the latest date you can order, as follows: requested receipt date - lead time calculation = order date. If you do not need delivery on a specific date, you can leave the field blank.

fields.name: Promised Receipt Date
fields.tooltip: Specifies the date that the vendor has promised to deliver the order.

fields.name: Lead Time Calculation
fields.tooltip: Specifies a date formula for the amount of time it takes to replenish the item.

fields.name: Inbound Whse. Handling Time
fields.tooltip: Specifies the time it takes to make items part of available inventory, after the items have been posted as received.

fields.name: Date Filter
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the date or time interval that values in quantity or amount fields are filtered by.
If this field contains starting and ending dates for a time interval, then the values in the quantity and amount fields will display the net change during that interval.
Enter a date or a time interval according to the following rules.


Meaning


Sample expression


Entries that are included


Equal to

12 15 12

Only entries that are posted on December 15, 2012.


Interval

12 15 12..01 15 13
..12 15 12

Entries that are posted on dates between and including December 15, 2012, and January 15, 2013.
Entries that are posted on January 15, 2012, or earlier.


Either/or

12 15 12|12 16 12

Entries that are posted on either December 15, 2012, or December 16, 2012. If there are entries that are posted on both days, then all entries are displayed.


You can also combine the various format types.


Sample expression


Entries that are included


12 15 12|12 01 12..12 10 12

Entries that are posted either on December 15, 2012, or on dates between and including December 1, 2012, and December 10, 2012.


..12 14 12|12 30 12..

Entries that are posted on December 14, 2012, or earlier, or entries that are posted on December 30, 2012, or later, which includes all entries except those that are posted on dates between and including December 15, 2012, and December 29, 2012.

fields.name: Vendor Authorization No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the identification number of a compensation agreement.

fields.name: Return Shipment No.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the number that is assigned to the return shipment when you post the purchase order.
The number is copied from the Return Shipment No. field on the purchase order line that represents the return shipment.

fields.name: Return Shipment No. Series
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the code for the number series that will be used to assign a number to the return shipment that will be created from the purchase document.
The program automatically copies the number series code from the Posted Return Shpt. Nos. field in the Purchases & Payables Setup table. You can change the code.

fields.name: Ship
fields.tooltip: Uses this field when posting.

fields.name: Last Return Shipment No.
fields.tooltip: This field is used internally. Tracks the last posted return shipment number that was used.

fields.name: Receipt on Invoice
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the receipt is posted on the invoice.

fields.name: Assigned User ID
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ID of the user who is responsible for the document.

fields.name: Doc. Amount Incl. VAT
fields.tooltip: Specifies the total amount (including VAT) of the purchase invoice or credit memo as specified in the external document. When this value comes from an e-document service, it's value can't be changed.

fields.name: Doc. Amount VAT
fields.tooltip: Specifies the VAT amount of the purchase invoice or credit memo as specified in the external document. When this values comes from an e-document service, it's value can't be changed.
